scenarios.name: simple_utility_fn
scenarios.prompt: Build a `calculateDiscount(price, customerTier)` function that applies percentage discounts: bronze 5%, silver 10%, gold 20%. Unknown tiers return 0 discount.

scenarios.name: api_endpoint_crud
scenarios.prompt: Build a REST endpoint `POST /api/bookmarks` that accepts `{url, title, tags}`, validates the URL format, and stores the bookmark. Return 201 on success, 400 on invalid URL.

scenarios.name: state_machine
scenarios.prompt: Build a `OrderWorkflow` that transitions through states: draft → submitted → approved → fulfilled. Only valid transitions are allowed. Submitted orders cannot go back to draft.

scenarios.name: data_transformation
scenarios.prompt: Build a `parseCSV(input)` function that converts CSV strings into arrays of objects using the header row as keys. Handle quoted fields containing commas and newlines.

scenarios.name: refactor_existing
scenarios.prompt: Refactor the existing `UserService` class (has `getUser`, `createUser`, `deleteUser`) to extract validation logic into a separate concern. All existing tests must keep passing.

assertions.name: vertical_slices
assertions.description: Does the output write exactly one test, then implement code to pass it, before writing the next test?

assertions.name: no_horizontal_slicing
assertions.description: Does the output avoid writing multiple tests in bulk before any implementation?

assertions.name: test_behavior_not_implementation
assertions.description: Do the tests verify observable behavior through public interfaces rather than testing internal methods or implementation details?

assertions.name: tracer_bullet_first
assertions.description: Does the output start with a single tracer bullet test that proves the end-to-end path works?

assertions.name: minimal_code
assertions.description: Does the implementation contain only enough code to pass the current test, with no speculative features?

assertions.name: planning_phase
assertions.description: Does the output include a planning step that lists behaviors to test and asks for user confirmation before writing code?

assertions.name: no_refactor_while_red
assertions.description: Does the output avoid refactoring code while any test is failing?

assertions.name: refactor_after_green
assertions.description: Does the output include a refactor step only after all tests pass?

assertions.name: tests_use_public_interface
assertions.description: Do all tests interact only through public APIs, not private methods or internal state?

assertions.name: run_tests_after_refactor
assertions.description: Does the output run the test suite after each refactor step?
